definition_of_done: Configuration supports three-layer architecture (keys, key instances, models) and can be loaded from the new config file format.
Multiple API keys per provider are supported with priority-based selection and failover across key instances.
Key rotation can occur without service restart and reflects in active routing behavior.
Backward compatibility is preserved for existing models_config.json users during migration.
Documentation is updated to describe the new configuration approach and migration notes.
non_goals: Refactoring unrelated routing logic or provider integrations.
Changing existing model pricing, rate limits, or routing policies beyond config schema support.
Building a UI for key management.
required_tests: Config loading/validation covers new schema plus legacy schema compatibility.
Runtime update path applies key rotation without restart and updates routing selection order.
Failover behavior selects the next eligible key instance when a key is unavailable or limited.
